purpose: A POS mismatch is a blocker. The dominant failure is putting a verb infinitive ('to ...') on a token whose QAC POS is a noun, proper noun, or adjective. Drop the verb gloss; author a nominal gloss only if a Qamus sense supports it, else pending. norm() never certifies POS — QAC POS leads.

fields.when: trigger condition over QAC POS / gloss shape
fields.then: the decision and what to do

rules.when: candidate gloss is a verb infinitive (English 'to ...' or an Arabic maṣdar-as-verb) AND qac_pos in ['N', 'PN', 'ADJ']
rules.then: DROP the verb gloss. Author a nominal gloss only if a Qamus sense supports the nominal use; otherwise leave the token plain and emit pending.
rules.note: This is sarf principle 3 — 'no verb gloss on a noun unless the Qamus sense supports the nominal use.'

rules.then: Never apply a verb gloss derived from the proper noun's root. A name is not its etymon. Prefer pending or a name-as-name record.

rules.when: wazn is an active/passive participle (ism fāʿil / ism mafʿūl) or a maṣdar AND qac_pos is nominal
rules.then: Use a nominal gloss (e.g. 'one who ...', '...ing', 'a ...'), never the bare verb infinitive whose sense differs.

rules.when: token is a maṣdar (verbal noun) AND a verb infinitive gloss is proposed
rules.then: Gloss as a noun ('freeing', not 'to free'). A maṣdar names the action; it is not the conjugated verb.

examples.why: root ر س ل; the token is a noun, not the verb أَرْسَلَ.
root ب ن و (noun 'son'), not ب ن ي (verb 'to build').
بَنِي إِسْرَائِيل = 'Children of Israel'; noun in iḍāfa, not the verb 'to build'.
proper noun; a name is not glossed by its root verb ح م د.
proper noun naming the Prophet; not the elative or the verb.

examples.wrong_gloss: the Prophet Ṣāliḥ / to be righteous
examples.why: active participle used adjectivally; not the proper noun and not a verb, unless context certifies the name.
